texto: I don't want to base my life on a symbol," he said resolutely. "I want reality, and the Christian faith has always been rooted in reality. What's not rooted in reality is the faith of liberal scholars. They're the ones who are following a pipe dream, but Christianity is not a pipe dream.

frase_original: I don't want to base my life on a symbol," he said resolutely. "I want reality, and the Christian faith has always been rooted in reality. What's not rooted in reality is the faith of liberal scholars. They're the ones who are following a pipe dream, but Christianity is not a pipe dream.

texto: If we further consider this divine panoramic view within which all evil is supposedly a "secret good" is held by a God who, according to Scripture, has a passionate hatred toward all evil, the "solution" becomes more problematic still. For it is certainly not clear how God could hate what he himself wills and sees as a contributing ingredient in the good of the whole. If all things play themselves out according to a divine plan, how can God genuinely hate anything?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Gregory A. Boyd que provoca temas ligados a espiritualidade. Ao questionar “If we further consider this divine panoramic view within which all evil is supposedly a "secret g…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre espiritualidade, evil, sovereignty-of-god, com foco em sovereignty-of-god e theodicy.
